name: Sierra Horconera – Pico Bermejo por el Puerto del Cerezo
region_intro: Hiking around Priego De Córdoba offers access to the diverse landscapes of the Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park, a UNESCO Geopark. The region is characterized by rugged limestone mountains, deep valleys, and extensive olive groves. Natural springs and rivers contribute to varied flora, while prominent features include the highest peak in Córdoba, La Tiñosa. This area provides a range of hiking experiences across its varied terrain. Best hiking trails around Priego De Córdoba The most popular hiking route is La Tiñosa Public Trail, a 7.8 miles (12.5 km) trail that takes 4 hours 27 minutes to complete. This moderate route ascends to the highest peak in the province of Córdoba, offering panoramic views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Sierra Horconera – Pico Bermejo por el Puerto del Cerezo, a difficult 8.7 miles (14.0 km) path. This trail features significant elevation gain through the rugged Sierra Horconera mountain range. Local hikers also love the Castle of Priego de Córdoba – Church of La Aurora loop from Priego de Córdoba, a 3.7 miles (5.9 km) trail leading through the historic town, often completed in about 1 hour 47 minutes. Hiking around Priego De Córdoba is defined by limestone mountains, deep river valleys, and vast olive groves. The network of routes offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Priego De Córdoba are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 1200 hikers have used komoot to explore Priego De Córdoba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Priego de Córdoba?
A: Priego de Córdoba offers nearly 20 hiking routes, catering to various skill levels. You'll find 8 easy, 8 moderate, and 3 difficult trails to explore the diverse landscapes of the Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Priego de Córdoba?
A: The terrain around Priego de Córdoba is highly varied, characterized by rugged limestone mountains, deep river valleys, and extensive olive groves. You'll encounter jagged mountain tops, dense forests, lush plateaus, and geological formations like dolines and poljes, especially within the Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Priego de Córdoba?
A: Yes, there are several easy routes suitable for families. For a pleasant stroll through the historic town, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14594730" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Priego de Córdoba – Church of La Aurora loop from Priego de Córdoba&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.7 miles long. The Vía Verde de la Subbética, an old railway track, also offers a flat and accessible path for all ages.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Priego de Córdoba?
A: Many trails in the Priego de Córdoba region are dog-friendly, especially those in natural park areas. It's generally recommended to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Always check specific trail regulations before heading out.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to see while hiking?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can explore the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5342358" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bat Cave&lt;/a&gt; near Zuheros, or witness the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1045186" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Limestone formations and caves in the Bailón Canyon&lt;/a&gt;. Don't miss the famous Fuente del Rey (King's Fountain) just outside Priego, known for its numerous natural springs.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in Priego de Córdoba are designed as loops. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38256953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Pico Bermejo – Puerto del Cerezo loop from Sierra de Pollos&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 11.1 km hike. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14594730" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Priego de Córdoba – Church of La Aurora loop from Priego de Córdoba&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 3.7-mile option.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Priego de Córdoba?
A: The best time for hiking in Priego de Córdoba is typically during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is mild and pleasant, and the landscapes are particularly vibrant. Summers can be very hot, making early mornings or late afternoons more suitable for hiking, while winters are generally mild but can be wet.

Q: Are there any historical sites or castles accessible from hiking trails?
A: Absolutely. The region is dotted with historical gems. You can visit the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5361618" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zuheros Castle&lt;/a&gt;, dramatically situated on a rock, which is integrated into the limestone landscape. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14594730" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Priego de Córdoba – Church of La Aurora loop from Priego de Córdoba&lt;/a&gt; also takes you through the historic town and past its castle.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Priego de Córdoba?
A: The hiking trails in Priego de Córdoba are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 200 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the well-maintained paths, and the stunning panoramic views, especially from higher elevations like La Tiñosa.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, the region offers difficult routes with significant elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995964266/sierra-horconera-pico-bermejo-por-el-puerto-del-cerezo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Horconera – Pico Bermejo por el Puerto del Cerezo&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 8.7-mile path through rugged mountain terrain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995984304/la-tinosa-public-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Tiñosa Public Trail&lt;/a&gt;, while moderate, ascends to the highest peak in Córdoba, offering a rewarding climb.

Q: Is public transport or parking available for accessing trailheads?
A: While specific public transport options to every trailhead may be limited, Priego de Córdoba itself is accessible by bus from larger cities. For many trailheads, especially those within the Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park, driving and parking are the most common ways to access them. Parking availability varies by location, so it's advisable to check local information for specific routes.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking routes that pass through the area?
A: Yes, the GR 7, a long-distance hiking route that crosses Spain, has a section that passes through the Priego de Córdoba region. This offers opportunities for multi-day treks and connecting with a wider network of trails.
tips: Pico Bermejo is the second highest peak in the province of Córdoba. There is no clear path to climb to the top, so there are several possibilities, some with a higher degree of difficulty like the one shown in the image. In some sections you can follow goat paths but in others the path is less clear and good orientation and skill in mountainous terrain are required.

With its 1474 m, this peak is the second highest in the province of Córdoba, located in the Sierra de Horconera in the Subbética Cordobesa. From the top you can see the Iznájar reservoir, the largest in Andalusia.

This place varies depending on the time of year you go and that is what makes it magical: if you go in spring, you will find a meadow full of flowers; if you go on a cloudy autumn day, you will see how the green of the grass becomes radiant against the gray sky; if you go in winter, it is possible that the drizzle and low clouds obscure your view; and if you go in summer, go very early, before the sun rises.
